results.title: Synology unveils next-generation DiskStation Manager, new AI-powered data management solutions at COMPUTEX 2026
results.description: As AI becomes part of everyday business operations, organizations are paying closer attention to how their data is stored, managed and protected. At COMPUTEX 2026, Synology unveiled innovative solutions across storage, data protection, surveillance, workplace collaboration and personal cloud services. Highlights include the next generation of DiskStation Manager (DSM), the latest ActiveProtect platform, new surveillance solutions, new additions to Synology Office Suite, and enhancements to the BeeStation private cloud lineup. "Data is at the heart of everything we build, and trust is the foundation," said Philip Wong, Chairman and CEO of Synology. "From enterprise infrastructure to personal cloud solutions, we develop every product to give users complete control over their data, backed by uncompromising security, reliability and privacy, so they can always embrace what's next with confidence." The next generation of DSM Built to be both AI-ready and enterprise-ready, the next generation of DiskStation Manager (DSM) introduces support for GPU NAS and AI appliances, enabling fully on-premises AI inference and governance. For larger deployments, Cluster Manager brings multiple systems together under a single interface. It supports workload migration, QoS, and centralized protection. Active Insight's new Mass Deployment feature enables rapid provisioning across multiple sites. A revamped Log Center gives IT teams better visibility into system activity to support security and compliance requirements. ActiveProtect Manager 2.0 and DP5200 ActiveProtect Manager (APM) 2.0 extends protection to AWS EC2, Azure VM, Proxmox, Nutanix AHV, and Google Workspace, allowing organizations to protect and recover data across cloud, virtualized, and SaaS environments from a single platform. The platform integrates AI-powered anomaly and malware detection to help identify potential threats earlier. The new DP5200 further expands the ActiveProtect lineup with additional deployment options for enterprises. New surveillance access control, DVA appliances, and hybrid cloud monitoring Synology broadens its surveillance ecosystem with new access control solutions, including the AC100 door controller and AR Series readers, new DC Series dome cameras, and new Deep Video Analytics (DVA) appliances. The latest DVA appliances introduce AI-powered tools that help security teams search video footage more quickly and track a person's movement across multiple cameras. Surveillance365 adds VSaaS capabilities that integrate with on-premises Surveillance Station, giving businesses a unified platform for remote and multi-site monitoring. Synology Office Suite expanded with ChatPlus and Meet Synology Office Suite adds ChatPlus and Meet, bringing enterprise-grade communication with granular permission and management controls. Both integrate AI-powered transcription and translation, helping teams work more efficiently while keeping communications data within the organization's environment. The additions expand Synology Office Suite's communication and collaboration capabilities. Bee Series private cloud ecosystem for individuals and homes The expanded BeeStation and BeeStation Plus lineup now offers a wider range of options to accommodate different storage needs. BeeCamera enables home monitoring on BeeStation Plus with Synology cameras, while Synology Deep Search uses AI-powered local search across personal content on macOS and Windows, helping users find content more quickly while keeping personal data private.

results.title: Decade-long national study finds antimicrobial use in Australian hospitals improves with regular monitoring
results.description: A 10-year national study has found improved antimicrobial use in Australian hospitals that regularly monitored their prescribing, using a globally unique program developed by a Melbourne hospital. The study reinforces the importance of antimicrobial stewardship to improve patient care and minimise antimicrobial resistance.

results.title: Pirates farm report for June 30, 2026: Callan Moss’ grand slam helps Curve outslug Portland
results.description: INDIANAPOLIS (Triple A, 35-47) thumped Omaha (Royals), 12-5. RF Dominic Fletcher (.289) hit a three-run homer. 3B Enmanuel Valdez (.244) smacked a three-run double. C Rafael Flores Jr. (.220) was 2 for 3 with two RBIs and three runs scored. DH Jhostynxon Garcia (.225) doubled in a run. RHP Khristian Curtis (6.39) started and gave up four runs on five hits with two walks and three strikeouts in 1 2/3 innings. LHP Nick Domkowski (3-4, 7.33) earned the win after giving up one unearned run on one hit with a walk and strikeout in 3 1/3 innings of relief.

results.title: Philip Morris International Gains First FDA MRTP Orders For ZYN Nicotine Pouches
results.description: Philip Morris International Inc. (PM) announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has issued Modified Risk Tobacco Product (MRTP) orders for 20 variants of ZYN nicotine pouch products, marking the first time nicotine pouches have received such authorization.

results.description: What is the Divine Feminine, and why has it been revered across ancient spiritual traditions? Discover how the concept of the Divine Feminine is understood in different traditions, its relevance in today's world, and why many seekers are rediscovering its wisdom. results.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Kylian Mbappe's Brace Fires France Past Sweden 3-0 To Reach Round Of 16 | Video
results.description: France stormed into the FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16 with a commanding 3-0 win over Sweden in New Jersey. Kylian Mbappe scored twice, taking his World Cup tally to 18 goals and moving to second on the all-time scoring list, while Bradley Barcola added another. Les Bleus will face Paraguay on July 5 as they continue their quest for a third consecutive World Cup final.
